1954996a-cff3-48c5-8af7-93490eb110e7.title: Render task references in chat messages
1954996a-cff3-48c5-8af7-93490eb110e7.details:
When a user sends a message containing `@task:<id>`, render it as a clickable/styled reference in the chat log so it's visually distinct from plain text (similar to how `@path` file mentions are rendered).

**Scope:**
- Parse `@task:<id>` patterns in user and assistant message content during rendering
- Render as a styled chip or link element (e.g., `<span class="chat-task-ref">`) showing the task title if resolvable, falling back to the ID
- Clicking the reference could navigate to the task view with that task selected

**Files to change:**
- `echo/frontend/src/app/chat/index.ts` â€” message rendering logic
- `echo/frontend/src/styles.css` â€” styling for the task reference element
acceptanceCriteria: @task:<id> patterns in chat messages render as styled references
Reference shows task title when resolvable from the current workspace's task board
Clicking a task reference navigates to task view with that task selected

21d15427-2d87-4e53-893a-dde9c06a9d8c.title: Render console output in chat tool calls (frontend)
acceptanceCriteria: renderToolCall displays consoleOutput in a <pre class="console-output"> block
Console output has terminal-style CSS (monospace, dark bg, scrollable)
wails generate picks up new ConsoleOutput field
npm run build passes

5ed1fe14-0d00-455d-a67f-d73b50f22edc.title: Add workspace activity summaries state and periodic refresh timer

startActivityRefreshTimer and stopActivityRefreshTimer exported from state.ts

68a000a9-1e40-4664-984a-82b6aa9840de.title: Render activity status dots and preview text on workspace dropdown options
acceptanceCriteria: Desktop workspace dropdown options show colored status dot + activity text
Mobile workspace dropdown options show the same indicators

Green pulsing dot indicates running kanban agents with count
Gray dot indicates idle workspace
Activity text shows streaming status, agent count, or last message snippet
CSS animations defined for pulse effect on active states
npm run build passes

772ab4be-3857-43c8-ad63-da9b58a16954.title: Fix mobile task view epic/tag filter bars layout
acceptanceCriteria: Epic filter bar scrolls horizontally without wrapping on mobile (â‰¤720px)
Tag filter bar scrolls horizontally without wrapping on mobile (â‰¤720px)
Filter labels never shrink or overlap with pills
Individual epic/tag pill buttons do not wrap their text
Task board displays in single-column layout on mobile
Hidden scrollbars maintain clean appearance while allowing touch drag
No regressions on desktop (>720px) task view layout

77591a51-2a2c-488b-be42-5fc1c21b0187.title: Add ConsoleOutput field to ChatToolActivity and populate for shell_command
77591a51-2a2c-488b-be42-5fc1c21b0187.details:
## Changes to `echo/internal/services/chat.go`

### 1. Add field to struct (line ~37)
```go
type ChatToolActivity struct {
    ID            string `json:"id"`
    Name          string `json:"name,omitempty"`
    Arguments     string `json:"arguments,omitempty"`
    Status        string `json:"status"`
    Result        string `json:"result,omitempty"`
    Error         string `json:"error,omitempty"`
    ConsoleOutput string `json:"consoleOutput,omitempty"`  // NEW
}
```

### 2. Populate in `executeToolCall` (line ~741)
After setting status to "running", emit a tool_event with preview for shell_command:
- Parse arguments JSON, extract `command` field
- Emit `ChatStreamEvent` with type `"tool_event"` and `ConsoleOutput: "âŹł Running: <command>"`

### 3. Populate on completion (line ~780)
After marshaling result to JSON, detect `shell_command`:
- Type-assert `result.Output` to a struct matching `shellCommandOutput` fields using map[string]any
- Build readable console string:
  ```
  > <command>
  <stdout lines>
  <stderr lines if any>

  exit code: X  duration: Yms
  ```
- Set `ConsoleOutput` on the activity in `updateToolActivity`

### 4. Update `updateToolActivity` signature and callers
Add `consoleOutput string` parameter; pass through from `executeToolCall`.

## Changes to `echo/internal/services/chat.go` - format helper
Add package-level helper:
```go
func formatShellConsoleOutput(cmd, stdout, stderr string, exitCode int, durationMs int64) string
```

## Verification
- `go test ./...` passes
acceptanceCriteria: ChatToolActivity has ConsoleOutput string field
shell_command running status emits âŹł preview with command text
shell_command complete status populates ConsoleOutput with stdout/stderr/exit code/duration
Non-shell tools leave ConsoleOutput empty

a0b22fe9-bf5d-4971-811f-3260e829f9c0.title: Regenerate Wails bindings for WorkspaceActivitySummary type and GetWorkspaceActivitySummaries method
a0b22fe9-bf5d-4971-811f-3260e829f9c0.details:
## Command

Run from repo root:
```powershell
wails generate
```

This generates the TypeScript type `services.WorkspaceActivitySummary` and the binding `SystemService.GetWorkspaceActivitySummaries` in `frontend/wailsjs/go/services/SystemService.ts` and `frontend/wailsjs/go/models.ts`.

## Verification
- `frontend/wailsjs/go/models.ts` has `WorkspaceActivitySummary` type with fields: `workspaceId`, `isChatBusy`, `isKanbanRunning`, `activeAgentCount`, `lastMessageSnippet`
- `frontend/wailsjs/go/services/SystemService.ts` has exported `GetWorkspaceActivitySummaries()` method
acceptanceCriteria: wails generate runs without errors
WorkspaceActivitySummary type present in frontend/wailsjs/go/models.ts
GetWorkspaceActivitySummaries binding present in frontend/wailsjs/go/services/SystemService.ts

GetWorkspaceActivitySummaries returns one summary per workspace reflecting current chat stream, kanban run, and agent state
LastMessageSnippet extracts last assistant message content trimmed to 60 chars with ellipsis
go test ./... passes

c406d766-e68a-45b8-b7d3-5b892df596bc.title: Show current tool call in dashboard kanban progress widget

d1d64412-6097-4429-94cc-fc3aa4272a25.title: Add "Reference Task" button to task detail view
d1d64412-6097-4429-94cc-fc3aa4272a25.details:
In `echo/frontend/src/app/tasks/index.ts`, add a new button in the `task-detail-footer-actions` div alongside the existing "Use as Chat Prompt" button.

**Button spec:**
- `data-task-action="reference"` with `data-task-id="${escapeAttribute(task.id)}"`
- Icon: link or paperclip icon (use existing icon from `icons.ts`)
- Label: "Reference Task"
- Class: `secondary-button icon-text-button` (match existing buttons in the footer)

**Handler** (new case in `handleTaskAction`):
- Call `formatTaskReference(task)` to produce a compact reference string like `@task:${task.id}`
- Append it to the existing chat draft (with leading space if needed, trailing space after) â€” do NOT replace existing draft
- Switch to chat mode and focus input (same pattern as existing `chat` action: set `appMode`, `mobileNavView`, `activeChatKanbanTab`, clear `selectedTaskCards`, render, focus `[data-chat-input]`)

**Helper function:**
```ts
function formatTaskReference(task: services.WorkspaceTask): string {
  return `@task:${task.id}`;
}
```

**Files to change:**
- `echo/frontend/src/app/tasks/index.ts` â€” button HTML, handler case, helper function
acceptanceCriteria: Reference Task button renders in task detail footer alongside Complete and Use as Chat Prompt
Clicking appends @task:<id> to existing chat draft without replacing it
View switches to chat mode and focuses the input textarea
Existing "Use as Chat Prompt" behavior remains unchanged

acceptanceCriteria: GetWorkspaceActivitySummaries function exported from services.ts using the call() pattern
Function signature matches generated Wails binding exactly
Works in both Wails runtime and web RPC mode
